title: Robo Rush 2026
summary: A 24-hour robotics sprint to build and race autonomous line followers.
description: Robo Rush is MARS CLUB's flagship annual challenge where teams design, assemble, and program autonomous bots under a fixed timeline. The final arena combines dynamic obstacles, turns, and speed checkpoints.
highlights: Live finals streamed to the main auditorium

title: Embedded Systems Bootcamp
summary: Hands-on workshop introducing microcontrollers, sensors, and firmware basics.
description: This 2-day bootcamp covers Arduino fundamentals, GPIO control, sensor interfacing, and real-world debugging workflows. Participants finish by building a mini automation prototype.
highlights: Starter kits provided to all attendees
Mini project showcase and peer review

title: CAD to Prototype
summary: From concept sketches to manufacturable models in a rapid design pipeline.
description: Students learn DFM principles, tolerance planning, and rapid prototyping. The session focuses on converting CAD assemblies into practical prototypes using available fabrication tools.

title: MARS Tech Summit
summary: A one-day summit on robotics innovation, startup journeys, and future skills.
description: MARS Tech Summit brings experts from robotics startups, research labs, and manufacturing leaders. Sessions include keynote talks, founder stories, and technical demos for students.
highlights: Panel on career pathways in robotics

title: AI for Robotics Lab
summary: Build perception pipelines for robots using computer vision and ML models.
description: Participants train and deploy lightweight vision models for object detection and basic navigation use-cases. The workshop emphasizes practical inference optimization on edge devices.
highlights: Live demo of autonomous object sorting

title: National Robotics Challenge
summary: Inter-college competition where MARS teams secured top podium finishes.
description: MARS CLUB represented the college at the National Robotics Challenge with teams competing in autonomous navigation and robotic arm precision tasks.
highlights: Special jury mention for system reliability
